content: I've been thinking about getting chickens for my backyard. I recently started beekeeping and love having animals around, but I'm not sure if chickens would work with my restaurant schedule.

Chickens can actually be quite low-maintenance once their coop is set up properly. They generally need feeding once a day and their eggs collected. How early do you typically start at your restaurant?
I'm usually there by 6am to prep for the day. The restaurant opens at 11am. I'm Logan, by the way, and I just opened my own place last year after leaving journalism.
Nice to meet you, Logan! Congratulations on the restaurant. Morning people like yourself are actually perfect for chicken keeping - you can let them out and check on them before heading to work.
That's good to know. I've got a pretty big garden where I grow mushrooms and vegetables. The chickens might actually help with pest control too.
Absolutely! Chickens are great for gardens - they eat bugs and their manure makes excellent compost. Plus, fresh eggs from your own backyard would be amazing for your restaurant.

Exactly what I was thinking! I'm 48 and this is my second career. Journalism was getting too stressful, and I've always loved cooking. Now I have more time for hobbies like photography and salsa dancing too.

It sounds like you've found a wonderful balance. Chickens would fit perfectly with your lifestyle - they're relatively quiet, don't need constant attention, and you can even teach them to come when called for treats.
I love that idea. I was also thinking about getting a dog eventually, but maybe I should start with chickens first. Do you think they'd get along?
Many people keep both successfully! You'd just need to train the dog properly and ensure the chickens have a secure coop. Some breeds are naturally better with chickens than others.
I'll definitely look into that. I've been reading philosophy books in my spare time too - right now I'm working through 'Being and Time' by Heidegger. It's challenging but fascinating.

Heidegger is definitely not light reading! It's great that you make time for intellectual pursuits alongside running a restaurant and your various hobbies. Your chickens will probably enjoy listening to you ponder existential questions while you collect eggs!
